x-deeplus-semantic-contract.event_identity: event_id is pairwise unique across every event-bearing field
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.sequence: sequence is pairwise unique and source_order equals event_id values sorted by ascending sequence
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.law_identity: every event law_id equals the enclosing record law_id
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.source_span: start is less than or equal to end for the record and every event
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.commit_point: null or the event_id of an ownership_commit event in ownership_events
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.stack_projection: each event-bearing field admits exactly the kinds named by x-deeplus-stack-kind-contracts
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.event_payload: each discriminated event contains every and only the common fields plus its kind-specific required payload
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.pattern_phase_order: one successful Pattern attempt is subject_evaluate, subject_acquire, test_plan_build, structural_test, probe_bind, optional guard_evaluate, atomic_commit, final_bind, body, exit_or_join; structural mismatch terminates after structural_test and false guard terminates after guard_evaluate, each followed only by exit_or_join, with no replay or backtracking
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.pattern_authority_binding: each phase names one exact DPM fixture, attempt disposition, and context; the fixture registry binds two records each for successful match commit, precommit mismatch, false guard, guarded-let else transfer, for-let filtering, and representative plain-let destructuring
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.pattern_failure_before_commit: mismatch or false guard leaves every precommit_zero_counts field and pattern_move_count at zero, creates no final binder, and executes only the context-bound failure disposition
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.pattern_guard_false: a false guard forbids atomic_commit, final_bind, and body events for that attempt
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.task_failure_order: at one aggregation scope barrier, a body failure remains primary; otherwise the minimum lexical spawn_index child failure is primary, sibling failures are suppressed in ascending spawn_index, and cleanup failures follow in actual LIFO execution order
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.task_order_non_evidence: scheduler completion order and source-file enumeration order are never failure-order evidence; the fixture registry includes two children that complete in reverse spawn order and a rejection case that incorrectly chooses the first completion
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.actor_fifo: actor_enqueue is an admitted ownership-commit event and assigns its non-null channel_sequence at that commit; the sequence is FIFO within the key sender_id, receiver_id, mailbox_profile_id, channel_id, and no cross-channel global order is promised
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.actor_transfer: a rejected precommit send is represented only by actor_lifecycle admission_rejected with null channel_sequence, ownership_commit_count zero, and sender_retained; a successful actor_enqueue has exactly one commit and receiver_owned or shared_evidence
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.actor_reply: one correlation_id has exactly one terminal reply, declared failure, or cancellation outcome
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.actor_request_task_responsibility: an actor_lifecycle enqueue_committed event with non-null correlation_id requires actor_request_task_responsibilities; the descriptor correlation_id values form a one-to-one set with distinct admitted-request correlation_id values in the record, while a record without such an admitted request has zero actor-request descriptors; every descriptor has result_type, normalized_handler_error_set, cancellation_axis, isolation_owner, correlation_id, and terminal_transport_failure={receiverClosedBeforeReply}; an ordinary async Task<T> does not carry this actor transport descriptor; await exposes normalize(normalized_handler_error_set | ActorMessageError::receiverClosedBeforeReply), while mailboxFull and receiverClosedBeforeAdmission remain admission Result errors and are forbidden in the descriptor
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.task_responsibility_compatibility: compatibility, join, storage, and API export preserve descriptor residue; static fields require exact normalized equality unless an explicit admitted error-set-subsumption proof widens normalized_handler_error_set, and each per-value correlation_id remains non-forgeable and unerased
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.actor_turn: for one actor_id/state_region_id the exact admitted lifecycle is turn_start, zero or more paired turn_suspend and turn_resume transitions that retain held state-region authority, then turn_finish/released before the next turn_start; overlapping turns are forbidden
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.shared_cell: withValue observe_begin/observe_end is nonconsuming and nonescaping; replace_commit is the single sequentially-consistent owner commit and returns the prior owner
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.shared_mutex: one lock_acquire owns one non-suspending scoped inout region and exactly one lock_release on every outcome; release handoff_index precedes the next acquire for the same sync_id
x-deeplus-semantic-contract.fixed_operator_conformance: an intrinsic-reserved pair has no fixed_operator_dispatches row; each admitted non-intrinsic binary +,-,* expression has one statically selected row whose identity fields survive HIR/MIR/API lowering and whose route, responsibility, runtime lookup and fallback constants are exact
